meta.description: Explore Lombok in 3 days — Gili Trawangan, Merese Hill sunset, Sasak village culture, Kuta Beach, and Banyumulek pottery. Private tour from RM688 per person. Book with Dhesu Travel.
meta.keywords: Lombok tour package Malaysia, 3 days Lombok, Gili Trawangan day trip, Merese Hill sunset, Sasak village tour, Lombok holiday from KL, Indonesia tour package

hero.title: 3 Days Lombok
hero.intro: Uncrowded beaches, raw volcano views, and laid-back island vibes — Lombok delivers everything Bali promises without the crowds. Explore Gili Trawangan, watch the sunset from Merese Hill, and discover authentic Sasak culture in a single unforgettable long weekend.
hero.heroImageAlt: Pristine white sand beach in Lombok with Mount Rinjani in the background

includes: All meals as specified (Day 1: Lunch & Dinner, Day 2: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner, Day 3: Breakfast)
excludes: Optional horse riding at Bromo (own expense)

overview.heading: The Perfect Long Weekend in Lombok
body: Lombok sits just east of Bali yet feels worlds apart — fewer tourists, rawer landscapes, and a culture shaped by the indigenous Sasak people over centuries. This 3-day private tour packs the island's greatest highlights into a seamless itinerary: the surfing beach at Kuta, the hilltop panorama at Merese Hill, the dazzling Gili Trawangan island, and the living craft traditions of Sukarara and Banyumulek villages.

All transport, meals, entrance fees, and your own English-speaking driver-guide are included. Simply fly in, and we handle the rest. The package is valid through March 2027, making it ideal for Malaysians planning a school holiday escape, honeymoon extension, or family adventure — all without the Bali crowds.

items.title: Gili Trawangan Island
items.body: A 15-minute boat ride delivers you to Lombok's most beloved tropical island — powdery white sand, crystal-clear water, and world-class snorkelling over vibrant coral reefs. No motorised vehicles, just pure island bliss.
items.imageAlt: Clear turquoise water at Gili Trawangan with white sand beach

items.title: Merese Hill Sunset
items.body: Perched above Kuta Bay, Merese Hill offers one of Indonesia's most photographed panoramas — rolling green hills sweeping down to twin turquoise bays. The golden hour here is simply unforgettable.
items.imageAlt: Panoramic sunset view from Merese Hill overlooking Kuta Bay, Lombok

items.title: Sasak Cultural Villages
items.body: Visit Sade Village, home to the indigenous Sasak tribe for over 15 generations, and Sukarara Village, where women weave intricate Songket fabric with gold and silver threads — a living tradition passed down through families.
items.imageAlt: Traditional Sasak village with thatched-roof houses in Lombok

items.title: Banyumulek Pottery Village
items.body: Watch master potters — 80% of whom are native Sasak — shape clay into beautiful decorative pieces using techniques unchanged for centuries. A perfect place to find authentic Lombok souvenirs.
items.imageAlt: Sasak pottery artisan crafting traditional terracotta at Banyumulek

days.title: Sasak Tribe > Kuta Beach > Merese Hill
days.description: Your guide greets you at Lombok International Airport and your island adventure begins immediately. After a welcome lunch at a local restaurant, head to Sade Village — a living museum of Sasak culture where 15 generations of the indigenous tribe have maintained their traditions in traditional thatched-roof homes. Next, discover Kuta Beach, Lombok's world-renowned surf break with dramatic headlands and turquoise water. Stop at the Mandalika International Street Circuit, venue for the MotoGP Indonesia Grand Prix. As the afternoon light turns golden, climb Merese Hill for a breathtaking panoramic view over Kuta Bay and Tanjung Aan Beach — arguably the finest sunset spot on the island. Round off the day with a visit to Sukarara Village to watch women weaving intricate Songket fabric with gold and silver threads, before a relaxed dinner and overnight at your Lombok hotel.
suggestedActivities: Photograph traditional Sasak architecture at Sade Village
Walk the golden sands of Kuta Beach
Watch sunset from Merese Hill viewpoint
Browse handwoven Songket textiles at Sukarara Village

days.title: Malimbu Hill > Gili Trawangan > Pusuk Pass
days.description: After breakfast, drive north to Malimbu Hill for sweeping coastal panoramas and coral reef views before reaching the pier for your 15-minute boat transfer to Gili Trawangan. Spend the heart of your day on this car-free island paradise — snorkel over coral gardens teeming with tropical fish, laze on the powder-white beach, or explore the island by horse cart. A fresh seafood lunch on the island keeps the energy up before the boat brings you back to the mainland. Stop at Pusuk Pass, a forested mountain road populated by playful long-tailed macaques and lined with pearl shops where you can pick up Lombok's famous freshwater pearls. Dinner at a local restaurant rounds off a perfect island day.
suggestedActivities: Snorkelling at Gili Trawangan coral reef
Beach walk and swimming on Gili T
Spot monkeys at Pusuk Pass mountain road

days.title: Lombok Souvenirs > Banyumulek Pottery > Departure
days.description: After a leisurely breakfast and hotel checkout, enjoy a final morning of souvenir shopping before visiting Banyumulek Pottery Village — one of Lombok's most distinctive crafts destinations, where 80% of residents are native Sasak artisans producing beautifully decorated terracotta pottery using traditional hand-shaping techniques. Pick up a piece to take home as a lasting memory of Lombok, then head to the airport for your departure flight.
suggestedActivities: Watch live pottery demonstrations at Banyumulek
Last-minute shopping for local snacks and batik

sections.heading: Lombok Tour Package from Malaysia — 3 Days 2 Nights
sections.body: Lombok is rapidly becoming Malaysia's favourite alternative to Bali. Just a short flight from Kuala Lumpur, this Indonesian island offers pristine beaches, an active volcano, and authentic Sasak culture at a fraction of Bali's prices. Our 3-day private tour is designed for Malaysian passport holders who want a fuss-free experience: no visa required for stays under 30 days, all transport included, and an English-speaking guide throughout. Whether you're planning a romantic getaway, a family trip during school holidays, or a quick adventure break, this package covers Lombok's top attractions at an unbeatable starting price of RM688 per person.

sections.heading: What to Expect on a 3-Day Lombok Island Tour
sections.body: In three packed days, you'll experience the cultural heart of Lombok at Sade Village and Sukarara weaving village, the natural drama of Merese Hill and Kuta Beach, a boat trip to the famous Gili Trawangan island for snorkelling and beach time, and the artisan heritage of Banyumulek pottery village. The tour is fully private, so your guide can adapt the pace to suit your group — whether you want more beach time on Gili Trawangan or longer stops at the craft villages. All meals listed in the itinerary are included, covering lunches and dinners at local restaurants where you'll sample authentic Lombok cuisine.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Lombok, Indonesia?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Indonesia visa-free for stays of up to 30 days. Simply ensure your passport is valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Lombok?
items.answer: Lombok's dry season runs from May to September and offers the calmest sea conditions for Gili Trawangan and the clearest views from Merese Hill. However, Lombok is a year-round destination — even during the wet season (November to March) showers are typically brief and mornings are often clear.

items.question: Are flights included in this package?
items.answer: No, flights are not included. We recommend flying from Kuala Lumpur (KUL) or Kuala Lumpur Subang (SZB) to Lombok International Airport (LOP). AirAsia operates direct flights several times weekly.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: This is a 100% private tour — you will not be sharing transport or a guide with other travellers. Your guide and vehicle are dedicated to your group only.

items.question: Can I extend this to 4 days?
items.answer: Yes! We offer a 4-day Lombok itinerary that adds iconic waterfall visits (Benang Stokel and Benang Kelambu), a trek to Sendang Gile waterfall near Mount Rinjani, and more village exploration. See our 4 Days Lombok Best-Kept Secret package starting from RM988.
